title: Translation Requests
wikitext:
[]: Translation Requests             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Mar 11, 2008 10:37 am Since there are quite a few important NGE resources that remain, so far as I know, untranslated, I think it might be useful to have a thread where we can post items that we would like to see translated, so that forum members that know Japanese (or at least think they know Japanese) can take a shot at them. To get the ball rolling, here's an item from Renewal vol. 7, DVD menu notes; #24', cut 279 (LILITH) [URL=http://img245.imageshack.us/my.php?image=002hl6.jpg] [/URL] Here's the related script: [URL=http://img385.imageshack.us/my.php?image=vlcsnap6431961zi8.png] [/URL] [URL=http://img291.imageshack.us/my.php?image=vlcsnap6432348yt7.png] [/URL] This is the famously problematic scene where Kaworu suddenly notices that "Adam" is actually "Lilith". I have wondered whether the DVD menu item has any useful info to contribute to our understanding of this matter. So, people, give it your best shot! (edit)BTW, here is the relevant page from the Newtype Film Book: [URL=http://img375.imageshack.us/my.php?image=evafilmbook09035ai0.jpg] [/URL] Unfortunately, I only have these crappy Chinese scans; perhaps Reichu could be so kind as to do a quick scan of the original Japanese page (we don't need the whole page really, just the Checkpoint at the bottom).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 11, 2008 1:12 pm Moved the thread since it'd get lost in Discussion easily, plus translations are kinda relevant to the Wiki. I can do scans, of course, but I can't guarantee when... My laundry list is getting rather long. I tend to do a whole shitload of scanning at once, so... I suppose I ought to create a thread appropriate for taking additional requests while I'm at it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 11, 2008 1:42 pm In regards to translation stuff: I already have it on my own to-do list have translating some Misato information of Chronicle (Reichu's way of testing me to see either how average or crappy my translating from Japanese really is), so I probably wouldn't be of much help there. (My reading/writing of Kanji is "made of fail", for one thing.) Might I recommend somebody like AchtungAffen or Brik, provided they'd be up to it, of course? From what I've seen, it seems like have lots of experience with Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Wed Mar 12, 2008 10:22 am I will be restarting my translation work quite soon after I am healed.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Wed Mar 12, 2008 10:50 am For me, I'm slowly coming along in Japanese, so I might come across something I can translate, but for now...not so much. I'll bet my kanji-fu is even weaker than SSD's. (I'll be able to make 10kyuu on the kanji kentei in about a month, as well as a few miscellaneous kanji) If it's in all kana, though, I might have a shot! (I could get one of Kaworu's lines! Souka. Sou iu koto ka, Ririn. lol) Basically, I'll try to do some as practice, but don't get your hopes up. Imperialist can pretty much tackle it all.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 12, 2008 12:02 pm You really don't have to know that many kanji. You just have to know how to look the things up / input them, really. Having text in "digitized" format makes things easier (plug them into a dictionary and get the definition, or whatever); I can apply OCR magic to any images that are 300dpi and up (which, unfortunately does not include the DVD scripts or menu notes -- though I do wonder how much the scripts in Evangelion Original have in common with the finals...).

[]:             Quote Post by AnonymousEvaTranslator » Wed Mar 12, 2008 3:39 pm As for the first image, it's rather unextraordinary. Basically it says that Kaoru saw the giant and called it "Adam." It says that the giant is actually Lilith, and goes over a brief detailing of the Hebrew tale of Lilith: First wife of Adam; God banished her; her children are called Lilin, etc. Nothing pertaining to the series really. The other pictures are basically synopsis of the scenes plus dialogue and sound effects, "pan up to Rei," "show shape of Terminal Dogma," and issuing the various sweeps with the camera [aori]. The only interesting thing is that the boxes for the various scenes refer to the giant as "Adam" rather than "Lilith." I could go into more detail, but eh... We've all heard it before. Edit: I have a habit of not reading what I write. Aori if anyone is interested is http://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%81%82%E3%81%8A%E3%82%8A_%28%E5%86%99%E7%9C%9F%29 Fukan is a similar skewed lens effect, but overhead. I think people on movie sets refer to aori as general camera movements--moving along with a scene. Actual translation in an hour or two. Must eat.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 12, 2008 3:47 pm @AET: Would it take too long to do a "more refined" translation of the DVD menu note? It's nothing special, yeah, but at least it's "one less batch of Moon Runes to wonder about" that I could put up on the site. The scripts are usually pretty boring, but every once in a while they say something interesting. We're eventually going to need to translate all of THOSE things, too. FYI, I believe aori refers to a type of camera angle, like one that's below eye level or something; the opposite is fukan .

[]:             Quote Post by AnonymousEvaTranslator » Wed Mar 12, 2008 6:01 pm DVD Menu: After seeing the underground giant, Kaworu referred to it as “Lilith.” Assuming the giant was Adam, he became aware that it was truly Lilith. According to the Hebrew story, Lilith was Adam's first wife. Because she would not submit to Adam during intercourse, she fled from him, and was punished by God. According to legend, after she fled, she is said to have had [lewd acts/sex] with demons, and gave birth to many children. Those children were Lilin.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Mar 12, 2008 11:21 pm @AET: Thanks. Must say, tho, that was a bit of a letdown. I hope at least some of these menu notes will prove to be more informative. BTW, that stuff about the story of Adam and Lilith sounds awfully familiar; I'm just about 100% sure that I've come across almost those exact words in some other (official?) NGE-related context...

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Mar 27, 2008 10:15 am One thing I hope to to accomplish with this thread is to get some of the Renewal DVD menu notes translated so that they can be included with the Commentary. Hopefully, when we get the Commentary going again, we will open up #01-04 for all the new people around here to add their input, and also add some new official material as it becomes available (= translated). With that in mind, here's some of Vol 01, ep01 pt A menu notes, with the bits of the Commentary that they go with: --- --- --- --- Well, I'll leave it at that for now, so as to not lay too much material at once on our noble translators. Please note the images of the DVD notes are at native resolution; I could upsize them, but that would merely make them bigger and fuzzier.

[]:             Quote Post by pmkava » Mon Apr 07, 2008 3:32 pm i've been getting in touch with some friends of mine who may be able to help with translations, it's really depends on if they have the free time to do it.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Apr 10, 2008 9:19 pm One thing I hope to to accomplish with this thread is to get some of the Renewal DVD menu notes translated so that they can be included with the Commentary.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Apr 11, 2008 7:22 am V wrote: One thing I hope to to accomplish with this thread is to get some of the Renewal DVD menu notes translated so that they can be included with the Commentary.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 11, 2008 7:41 am Shin-seiki wrote: Try asking Reichu, since she probably has them all capped and archived.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Apr 11, 2008 1:00 pm Decided to kill some time by translating this episode summary from the German edition of 'Evangelion Newtype 100% Collection': Newtype 100% wrote: A young boy runs alone through a city. His name is Shinji Ikari. He has been summoned, without any explanation, by his father to Neo-Tokyo 3. At the same time an enigmatic gigantic object appears in Japan, a so-called "Angel", like was last seen 15 years ago. The army tries to fend off its attack, but can hardly do any damage, therefore the strategic direction of the UN forces transfers over to the Commander of the secret [organization] NERV. This is none other than Gendo Ikari, Shinji's father... As Shinji enters NERV headquarters in the Geo-Front (an underground city), where his father awaits him, he is confronted for the first time with NERV's all-purpose weapon: Evangelion! (EVA for short.) Gendo orders Shinji to get aboard the EVA, [to] counter the approaching Angel. Shinji initially refused because he now realizes that he was only summoned in order to serve as pilot of the robot. When he sees that a girl of his same age, Rei Ayanami, is willing to pilot the EVA despite her injuries, he decides then to get in the EVA himself instead. And so the Prototype, under Gendo's supervision, sets forth on the attack.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Apr 11, 2008 5:24 pm Reichu wrote: Shin-seiki wrote: Try asking Reichu, since she probably has them all capped and archived.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 14, 2008 5:31 pm A young boy runs alone through a city. His name is Shinji Ikari.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Aug 14, 2008 9:40 am I guess there is nothing to translate right now...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 14, 2008 9:54 am The Imperialist wrote: I guess there is nothing to translate right now...
